decision.reason: The finite D20 mask table is now fenced as a testbed and local gadget source, not an asymptotic SAT-complete target. Full bridge closure requires an assignment-bearing parameterized e33 target family with forward and inverse witnesses.

evidence.finite_target_relation: nonzero sector-33 height residual after public CNF fingerprint compilation
evidence.finite_target_scope: fixed 11-bit D20 mask / sector-33 residual lookup
evidence.meaning: This check applies only to the earlier fixed finite D20 target. A separately certified parameterized assignment-bearing target does not change the finite lookup collapse.
The height/action-return transport is present; what is missing is the assignment-bearing target relation that makes the transport semantic for SAT.

evidence.decider: mask in certified finite table and residual_integral != 0
evidence.lookup_bound: O(1) after the 2048-row certificate is loaded

finite_target_collapse.summary: A target predicate depending only on the fixed 11-bit D20 mask is a finite lookup problem. As a separation route, that is the wrong target: a many-one reduction from SAT to this finite language would put SAT in P by running the compiler and table lookup, while the tested nonzero-residual predicate already fails on UNSAT canaries.

formula_compiler_candidate.output_size_bound: at most 11 primitive D20 cycles plus one certified finite transport row

minimum_acceptance_tests_for_next_artifa: schema names an unbounded parameter n/m/L rather than a fixed D20 mask table
compiler emits target packet from DIMACS only and records no solver outcome
witness relation carries assignment bits and local clause selectors
rho_33 transport is recomputed from emitted edge or circuit data
SAT canaries and UNSAT canaries exercise both completeness and soundness
inverse witness projection recovers a satisfying assignment from every accepting target witness
all size and replay bounds are polynomial in the source formula size

next_highest_yield_item.action: Refresh the full no-escape closure ledger against the certified encoded-family reduction.

non_claims: This does not construct the parameterized target family.
This does not prove that every possible finite fingerprint fails unless P != NP is assumed.
This does not prove P != NP.

assignment_bearing_witness_relation.shape: Witnesses must contain assignment bits a in {0,1}^n plus local clause/literal selectors, so accepting target witnesses project back to satisfying assignments.

clause_local_soundness_gates.shape: Each clause gadget must expose a public local check that at least one selected literal is true under the carried assignment.

forall_yes_no_preservation.shape: Completeness: a satisfying assignment for phi builds an accepting target witness. Soundness: any accepting target witness projects to a satisfying assignment for phi.

intrinsic_height_action_return_transport.shape: rho_33(E(phi), a) must be derived as -A_h(C(phi, a))/dim(Pi_33) e_33 from emitted edge or basis-cycle circuit data; no certified residual scalar may be inserted.

inverse_witness_projection.shape: The target witness must include enough public support data to recover the CNF assignment, rather than only a hidden-sector residual bit.

parameterized_unbounded_instance_family.shape: Target instances must be indexed by formula size parameters such as variables n, clauses m, and literal occurrences L; the support cannot be the fixed 2048-mask table.

polynomial_size_and_replay_bounds.shape: The target packet, witness, local replay checks, and height transport must have size and verification cost polynomial in n + m + L.

public_cnf_to_target_packet_compiler.shape: A deterministic public compiler maps DIMACS CNF phi to a target packet E(phi) without solver outcome, hidden e33 data, or X-extractor advice.
